doc.title: Counterbalancing error — inventory misstatement
doc.event: Hoyt Co. discovers, during year 2, that last year's ENDING inventory was overstated by $25,000 (periodic system; year 2 not yet closed). Analyze the effect on each year and prepare the correcting entry.

entries.caption: Correcting entry, discovered in year 2 (books open)

lines.label: year 1's overstated income
lines.why: Year 1's overstated ending inventory understated COGS → overstated income → that error now sits in beginning RE. Prior-period adjustment pulls it out.
lines.trap: Errors are neither principle nor estimate — always a restatement via RE, never through current income.

lines.label: year 2's inflated COGS
lines.why: The same overstated figure rolled in as year 2's BEGINNING inventory, inflating this year's COGS by 25,000 — the credit fixes the current year before it closes.
lines.trap: Both sides need fixing when caught mid-seesaw: RE for last year, COGS for this year. Fixing only one leaves half the error alive.

computationPaths.narration: The counterbalancing pattern: ending inventory overstated → year-1 income OVER by 25,000 → same figure becomes year-2 beginning inventory → year-2 income UNDER by 25,000 → retained earnings is correct again after two full years. Discovered AFTER year 2 closes: no entry needed (RE already right) — restate the comparatives only.

memorize.body: Inventory-error compass: ending inventory OVER → income OVER (that year) and UNDER (next year). Counterbalancing errors self-correct in RE after two years; the correcting entry depends entirely on WHEN you catch it.
Same seesaw family: accrued expenses/revenues omitted at year-end, prepaids expensed early. All counterbalance in two years. The exam's question is always 'when was it found?'

questions.prompt: Year-1 ending inventory was overstated by 25,000; the error is discovered AFTER year 2 has closed. The correcting journal entry is…

distractors.feedback: After two full years a counterbalancing error has already washed out of RE — no entry exists to make. Restate the prior-year statements presented; the books themselves are fine.

build.scaffold: Trace the seesaw first (which years, which direction), then ask when it was caught: mid-seesaw → fix RE and the current-year account; after it closed → no entry, restate only.
